scope.summary: Seed the canonical default trim library: author and run a migration (or seeder) that inserts a minimum set of is_library_item=TRUE rows in tailor_trims covering every trim_category value in the CHECK domain (button, zipper, eyelet, rivet, hook_eye, snap, buckle, strap_segment, armor_plate, accessory), each with valid mesh_json, inertia_tensor_json, and tack_anchor_json
acceptance_criteria: After running migrations, tailor_trims contains at least one row with is_library_item=TRUE for each trim_category CHECK value defined in TR-6.1.2
Every library row has non-null mesh_json (TrimMeshV1), non-null inertia_tensor_json, and non-null tack_anchor_json with at least one anchor position
The sandbox adapter can resolve a library item for every trim_category without an explicit trim_id per TR-9.3: passing trim_category='button' returns the seeded button row
Library rows are idempotent: re-running the migration or seeder does not produce duplicate rows (ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING or equivalent)
Seeded inertia tensors are positive-definite (det(I) > 0) for all library items
scope.risk_if_missed: TR-9.3 is a hard requirement: every model-authored GarmentSpec that references a trim_category without a trim_id will fail at sandbox time with a missing-row error; the place_trim MCP tool (MT-263) becomes unusable in the primary model authoring path since models cannot be expected to know internal TRIM- UUIDs

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
